image:image.image:title: Attica - Greek Beaches
image:image.image:caption: Attica, Greece - The Attica coastline is surprisingly diverse, offering everything from organized city beaches to secluded coves. The water is generally clear and deep, typical of the Aegean Sea. Attica is the historical and administrative region of Greece that houses the capital city, Athens. It's a peninsula jutting into the Aegean Sea, defined by its dramatic landscape.

image:image.image:title: Chalkidiki - Greek Beaches
image:image.image:caption: Chalkidiki, Greece - Famous for its three peninsulas stretching into the Aegean—Kassandra, Sithonia, and Mount Athos—Chalkidiki blends pine forests with turquoise waters and golden beaches. Known for both lively resorts and hidden coves, it offers a mix of family-friendly coastlines, unspoiled natural escapes, and cultural heritage rooted in ancient Greece and Byzantine monasteries.

image:image.image:title: Corfu - Greek Beaches
image:image.image:caption: Corfu, Greece - Corfu, or Kérkyra, is a lush Greek island in the Ionian Sea, known for its stunning natural beauty with green landscapes and beaches, as well as a unique culture shaped by centuries of Venetian, French, and British rule. The island features the historic, cosmopolitan Corfu Old Town (a UNESCO site), with its fortress architecture, alongside traditional villages, olive groves, and popular tourist resorts.

image:image.image:title: Crete - Greek Beaches
image:image.image:caption: Crete, Greece - Crete is Greece's largest island and a major Mediterranean destination, famed for its rich history, including the ancient Minoan civilization centered at Knossos, its stunning diverse landscapes of mountains and beaches, and its unique culinary traditions.

image:image.image:title: Lefkada - Greek Beaches
image:image.image:caption: Lefkada, Greece - Lefkada is a Greek island in the Ionian Sea, known for its dramatic landscapes with towering white cliffs and stunning turquoise beaches like Porto Katsiki and Egremni, making it a popular vacation spot. Unlike most other Greek islands, it's connected to the mainland by a floating bridge, providing easy road access for visitors.

image:image.image:title: Milos - Greek Beaches
image:image.image:caption: Milos, Greece - Milos is a volcanic Greek island in the Cyclades group, famous for its unique volcanic landscape with colorful cliffs, caves, and over 70 diverse beaches like the famous Sarakiniko.

image:image.image:title: Mykonos - Greek Beaches
image:image.image:caption: Mykonos, Greece - Mykonos is a glamorous Greek island known for its iconic white-washed architecture, famous windmills, and stunning beaches, offering a mix of vibrant nightlife and a more serene, traditional side with picturesque alleys and ancient sites nearby.

image:image.image:title: Naxos - Greek Beaches
image:image.image:caption: Naxos, Greece - Naxos is a large, green island in Greece's Cyclades group, known for its beautiful sandy beaches, charming mountain villages, fertile agricultural land, and ancient ruins like the Portara. As the largest Cycladic island, Naxos offers a varied experience, from relaxing on the coast and enjoying delicious local food to exploring archaeological sites and hiking mountainous trails.

image:image.image:title: Paros - Greek Beaches
image:image.image:caption: Paros, Greece - Paros is a popular Greek island in the central Aegean Sea, known for its picturesque Cycladic villages, golden beaches, vibrant nightlife, and mix of traditional and modern attractions, offering a balanced experience for relaxation and adventure.

image:image.image:title: Santorini - Greek Beaches
image:image.image:caption: Santorini, Greece - Santorini is a world-famous Greek island in the Aegean Sea, known for its breathtaking caldera views, picturesque villages with whitewashed buildings, and stunning sunsets. Formed by a massive volcanic eruption thousands of years ago, it features dramatic cliffs, unique volcanic beaches, and historical sites like the ruins of Akrotiri.

image:image.image:title: Zakynthos - Greek Beaches
image:image.image:caption: Zakynthos, Greece - Zakynthos (also known as Zante) is a verdant Greek island in the Ionian Sea, famous for its diverse landscapes, from dramatic cliffs and rocky western coasts to sandy eastern beaches.
